poem.content:
The hospital clock, a slow indictate of dread,
Each hour a hammer blow upon my head.
A myth spun of healing, a white walled lie,
While shadows gathered, and hope began to die.

A loud insistent call, a surgeon's drill,
To sample frail flesh, against my will.
Muscles bound so tight, a painful bind,
Leaving all peace and solace far behind.

Later, escaping south, a cabin small,
Where I recall the sight, the ghostly thrall.
The wind will reopen wounds, once neatly sealed,
A pain now known, a truth at last revealed.

I try to drill deep, find strength to mend,
Before this anguish brings about my end.
The muscles ache, a ghostly, haunted shore,
Where healing waits, or death will evermore,

Bind my soul, to shadows left behind,
And indicate there's not much to find.
